target_db: Customer ExaCS PDB DB0401_PDB_JLL.paas.oracle.com running Oracle 23ai 23.26.1.0.0
auth_mechanism: Plain user/password on TCP 1521; server-enforced Oracle Native Network Encryption (NNE)
scan_proxy: PE-ARCH 3c (RCE with SCAN Proxy) — workspace networkConfigurationDetails.scanDetails registered jll-scan-oopzk fqdn:1521
smoke_query: SELECT network_service_banner FROM v$session_connect_info WHERE sid = SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV','SID')
skill_alignment: Skill aidp-exacs Option A (recommended default) is now built on this exact pattern: plain TCP 1521 + spark_jdbc_options_password() + server-enforced NNE. Wallet TCPS demoted to Option B. examples/exacs_user_password.ipynb mirrors this notebook (DNS check + TCP + JDBC + NNE verify) and is the env-var-driven canonical version.
notes: Live evidence from a customer-tenancy ExaCS deployment. End-to-end Spark JDBC connection succeeded; AES256 in-transit encryption confirmed via v$session_connect_info banners. The reference demo notebook (`exacs_intransit_encryption_demo.ipynb` in workspace `exacs-private-test`) ran cleanly with row count and encryption confirmed. The plugin's example notebook for row 6 is a parameterized version of the same pattern.
